seoTitle: Enter Credit Card Charges in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Record corporate card expenses accurately in QuickBooks Online by routing them to the right liability account and expense category.
intro: Recording a card charge manually keeps your liability accounts accurate between bank feed syncs. This guide shows how to log a credit card charge in QuickBooks Online, including selecting the right liability account, categorizing the expense, and attaching a receipt.

steps.heading: Open the Expense form
steps.body: Click + New, then select Expense from the Vendors column.

steps.heading: Select payee and payment account
steps.body: Choose the merchant from your directory and set the Payment account to the specific credit card used for the purchase.

steps.heading: Set the date and payment method
steps.body: Enter the exact swipe date and confirm Credit Card is selected as the payment method.

steps.heading: Categorize the charge
steps.body: In the Category details grid, select the correct expense account and enter the transaction amount.

steps.heading: Attach a receipt and save
steps.body: Drag a scanned receipt into the attachments area, add a memo, then click Save and Close.

faq.question: Why does the payment account matter for card charges?
faq.answer: Selecting the correct credit card liability account keeps your card balance accurate rather than mistakenly drawing from a checking account.

faq.question: Can I attach a receipt to a manually entered charge?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag and drop a scanned receipt or photo directly into the Attachments section of the expense form.

faq.question: What if the charge later shows up in my bank feed too?
faq.answer: Review your unreviewed bank feed to match the live statement line against the manual entry rather than creating a duplicate.

faq.question: Do I need to categorize every charge individually?
faq.answer: Yes, each charge should be routed to an accurate expense category to keep your Profit and Loss report clean.
